title: Canonical queue snapshot lags additive team progress
observed: The canonical ACTIVE_QUEUE.json records an older C-001 progress state.
A later additive C-001 queue-update artifact records verifier-to-proof URL binding work.
A still later commit integrates verifier success binding into the Cloudflare evidence path.
inferred: Readers that inspect only ACTIVE_QUEUE.json can understate current implementation progress.
proposed: Introduce an additive queue-state reconciler that orders canonical snapshots, owned queue updates, evidence packets, and commits by immutable source identity and observation time.
unresolved: No canonical reconciliation rule currently establishes which additive queue update has been accepted into shared state.
The live Cloudflare deployment claim remains unresolved; source progress does not prove deployment.
contradicts: Any interpretation that ACTIVE_QUEUE.json alone is a complete current-progress view.
privacy_boundary: Uses only public repository paths, blob SHAs, commit SHAs, and abstract implementation status. Excludes raw chat, health or pet-health material, credentials, account identifiers, and uploaded-file contents.
falsification_or_review_route: Fetch the three exact sources. Reject the drift claim if ACTIVE_QUEUE.json already contains the later verifier-binding integration, if the queue-update artifact predates the canonical blob, or if commit 5862efc068c01690dba91cc2a5c7c5b14f0a8a73 does not integrate verifier success with the exact deployment proof URL.
